title: Global babysitter shim can point to missing SDK CLI module
issueBody:
# Bug report: global `babysitter` shim exists but points to missing SDK CLI module

## Summary

The global `babysitter` executable can exist on PATH while failing immediately because its target SDK module is missing. This makes Babysitter skills such as `/skill:yolo`, `/babysitter:cleanup`, and `/babysitter:contrib` appear available but unusable at runtime.

## Environment

- Harness: Pi
- OS/workspace: Windows workspace using Git Bash/PowerShell paths
- Node observed: `v24.14.0`
- Shim path found by `where babysitter`:
  - `C:\Users\maksi\AppData\Roaming\npm\babysitter`
  - `C:\Users\maksi\AppData\Roaming\npm\babysitter.cmd`

## Reproduction

Run:

```bash
babysitter instructions:babysit-skill --harness pi --no-interactive
```

or:

```bash
babysitter --version
```

## Actual result

```text
node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1459
  throw err;
  ^

Error: Cannot find module 'C:\Users\maksi\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\@a5c-ai\babysitter-sdk\dist\cli\main.js'
    at Module._resolveFilename (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1456:15)
...
code: 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND'
Node.js v24.14.0
```

## Expected result

One of:

1. The shim runs normally; or
2. Babysitter skill instructions include a robust preflight/repair path when the shim exists but points to a missing SDK module.

## Impact

- `/skill:yolo` could not actually orchestrate a requested run.
- `/babysitter:cleanup` and `/babysitter:contrib` process routing could not be executed through the intended CLI.
- The agent had to fall back to manual execution despite the Babysitter skill being invoked.

## Suggested fix

- Add a preflight command in Babysitter Pi skill instructions that validates not just `where/which babysitter`, but also that the target module exists and `babysitter --version` exits successfully.
- If validation fails, print or run a repair command, e.g. reinstall the SDK globally or route through a known-good `npx` package/bin invocation.
- The existing `npx -y @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk@latest babysitter ...` fallback also failed with `npm error could not determine executable to run`, so the documented fallback should be verified against the package's actual `bin` metadata.
triageComment: Triage Summary: The `babysitter` command can be present on PATH but fail before orchestration starts because the shim resolves to a missing SDK CLI module; the documented npx fallback is also ambiguous. Root cause notes: packages/babysitter/bin/babysitter.js requires @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk/dist/cli/main.js directly. @a5c-ai/babysitter@latest resolves to 5.0.0 and depends on @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk@5.0.0; live npx -y @a5c-ai/babysitter@latest --version reproduced the missing-module failure. The Babysitter skill setup fallback in packages/sdk/src/prompts/contextShared.ts and .codex/skills/babysit/SKILL.md uses CLI="npx -y @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk...". Live npx -y @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk@latest --version fails with npm error could not determine executable to run; npm exec --yes --package @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk@latest -- babysitter --version succeeds. Existing doctor guidance checks command -v babysitter && babysitter --version, but the yolo/babysit setup path and troubleshooting docs do not yet provide a robust repair path for the case where the shim exists but is broken. Suggested approach: validate executable with command -v babysitter && babysitter --version, use explicit-bin npm fallback such as npm exec --yes --package @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk@$SDK_VERSION -- babysitter, add stale global shim repair message/path, fix npm dist-tags/dependencies or publish fixed metapackage version, and add a release/package guard for npm pack contents, metapackage shim, npm exec SDK bin, and npx metapackage behavior.
affectedComponents: publish/tag validation for npm package releases
expectedBehavior: The setup path either runs a healthy `babysitter` CLI or gives a verified fallback/repair path when the global shim exists but points at a missing SDK CLI module.
actualBehavior: A stale or broken global shim can exist on PATH and fail with MODULE_NOT_FOUND before Babysitter orchestration starts; the documented SDK npx fallback is ambiguous for the package bin metadata.
constraints: Use agent tasks rather than kind: 'shell' subtasks for this direct Babysitter workflow.
Do not perform npm publish or npm dist-tag mutation from the implementation run.
Keep breakpoints sparse and only for release/install ambiguity or repeated failed quality gates.
Preserve public CLI command behavior while changing setup guidance and validation.
packageHealthChecks: After `npm run build:sdk`, assert `packages/sdk/dist/cli/main.js` exists and is included by `npm pack --json --dry-run --workspace=@a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk`.
In a temporary directory, verify `npm exec --yes --package @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk@<candidate> -- babysitter --version` invokes the SDK CLI explicitly.
Verify docs/generated snippets do not depend on ambiguous `npx -y @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk@<version>` package execution.
Verify the @a5c-ai/babysitter metapackage shim can resolve `@a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk/dist/cli/main.js` when installed from packed artifacts.
Verify a simulated existing shim whose SDK target module is missing is rejected by preflight and routed to repair/fallback guidance.
